column2: And David said  to all the assembly of Israel, If it seems good to you, and by the LORD our God, that he should prosper the way, then we should send to  our brethren  being left in all the land of Israel, and the priests the Levites with them which are in the cities of their possession, that they shall be gathered together to us.
And Eleazar died,  and there were no  sons to him, but only daughters. And the sons of Kish their brethren took them.
To Mahli -- Eleazar, and there were no  sons to him.

And David said  to Solomon  his son,  Be strong and manly, and act! Do not fear nor be terrified! for the LORD my God is with you. He will not send you away, and in no way will abandon he you until  you complete  every ministration of work of the house of the LORD.
And the ones who were found with stones for themselves gave to the storehouses of the house of the LORD, through the hand of Jehiel the Gershonite.

And his servants said   to him, Let them seek  for our master  the king a young virgin, and she shall stand beside the king, and will be warming him, and she shall recline in  his bosom,  and our master the king shall be heated.
And Nathan said,  O my master,  O king, have said you, Adonijah shall reign after me, and he shall sit upon my throne?
And the king responded and said, Give the living child  to the one having said, Give to it her! and unto death you should not put it to death --  she is  his mother.

And all Israel knew that the king did not hearken to them.  And the people answered to the king, saying, What is our portion with David? and, There is no  inheritance to us with the son of Jesse. Run, O Israel, to  your tents!  Now graze  your own house,  David! And Israel went forth  to  his tents.
and he went after the man  of God. And he found him sitting down under the oak. And he said to him, Are you the man  of God, the one coming from out of Judah? And he said, I am.

And it was not  enough for him  to go in the sins of Jeroboam son of Nebat, but he took for wife Jezebel the daughter of Ethbaal king of the Sidonians. And he went and served to Baal, and did obeisance to him.
And Elijah yelled out  to the LORD, and he said, O LORD  my God,  also indeed  the widow with whom I dwell with her,  have inflicted evil you  to kill  her son?
And  to Hannah he gave one portion, for there was no  child to her; except that  Elkanah loved Hannah  above Peninnah. But the LORD locked the matter concerning  her womb.
And of their coming unto  Zuph, and Saul said to his servant-lad   with him, Come, for we should return, lest my father sparing   concerning the donkeys, should be thoughtful  concerning us.
And they ascend into the city. And they were entering in the midst of the city, and behold, Samuel came forth for the meeting them,  to ascend unto Bama.
And Nahash the Ammonite said to them, By this I will ordain with you a covenant, in the gouging out of all of you the right eye, and I will make it for scorn upon all Israel.

And Jonathan said  to David, Go in peace! And as we swore by an oath, both of us in the name of the LORD, saying, The LORD will be witness between me and you, and between  my seed and  your seed,  unto the eon. And David rose up  and went forth. And Jonathan entered into the city.

And Saul said to him, Why did you agree against me, you and the son of Jesse,  for you to give  him bread, and a broadsword, and should ask on account his  of God,  to put him against me for an enemy, as he is  this day?
And now hear indeed the voice of your maidservant, for I will place before you a morsel of bread and you eat! and it will be for strength to you, that you should go in the way.

And David said to him, Who are you, and from what place are you? And the Egyptian servant-lad said,  I am a servant of a man, an Amalekite; and my master left me   for I was unwell,  today being three days.
But David brought the ark of God   from out of the city of Kirjath Jearim, for David prepared for it; for he pitched a tent for it in Jerusalem.
In  that night God appeared to Solomon, and he said to him, Ask what shall give to I you!
then shall listen you from the heaven, and you should be propitious  to the sins  of your people Israel, and shall return them into the land which you gave to them and to their fathers.
And if you should go before me as David your father went,   and should do according to all what I gave charge to you, and  keep my orders and my judgments;

And they burn to the LORD whole burnt-offerings morning by morning, and  evening to evening, and they have the incense composition, and place settings of bread loaves upon the pure table,  and the gold lamp-stand,  and  its lamps   to light  evening by evening. For we guard the watch of the LORD, the God  of our fathers; but you abandoned him.

And Asa pursued them and  his people  unto Gerar. And the Ethiopians fell so as to not be in procurement of safety for themselves; for they were defeated before the LORD, and before  his force.  And they despoiled many spoils.

And it came to pass after the coming of Amaziah, having struck the Edomite, that he brought to them the gods of the sons of Seir, and he set them up to himself as gods; and he did obeisance before them, and sacrificed to them.
and among the cities of Manasseh, and Ephraim, and Simeon, and Naphtali, and the places round about them.
And Elijah said,  You hardened  to ask. If you should behold me being taken up from you, it will be thus to you; but if  not, in no way will it be.
And it came to pass of going their and speaking, that behold, a chariot of fire, and horses of fire, and they drew apart between both; and Elijah was taken up  in a rumbling unto into the heaven.
In  his days  Pul king of the Assyrians ascended upon the land. And Menahem gave  to Pul a thousand talents of silver  for his hand to be  with him,  to strengthen  his kingdom  in hand his.
Thus says the king, Let not Hezekiah lift you! for in no way should he be able to rescue you from out of my hand.
Behold, you heard all as much as the king of the Assyrians did in all the lands, as they utterly destroyed them; and how shall be rescued you?
And David said to him, From what place do come you? And he said to him, From out of the camp of Israel I have come through safe.
And Abner looked  to  his rear,  and said, Are you Asahel himself? And he said, I am.
And the elders of his house rose up   to him,  to raise him from the ground, and he did not want, and he would not eat bread with them.
And David sent  for Tamar to the house, saying, Go indeed to the house of Amnon  your brother,  and make food for him!
And the servants of Absalom did  to Amnon as he gave charge to them. And all the sons of the king rose up, and each mounted upon  mule his, and they fled.

And Absalom rose early, and stood at the way of the gate. And it came to pass every man in which had a case, and came to the king for a judgment, that Absalom called him, and said to him, From out of of what city are you? And he said, your servant is from one of the tribes of Israel.

And David said  to all his servants  with him in Jerusalem, Rise up! for we should flee, for there is no  deliverance for us from the face of Absalom. Hasten  to go! that he should not hasten and overtake us, and thrust evil upon us,  and should strike the city by the mouth of the broadsword.
And the king said to Amasa, Call to me men of Judah in three days, but you stand here!
I am peaceable of ones supporting Israel. But you seek to kill a city, even a mother-city in Israel. Why do you swallow down the inheritance of the LORD?
And the Gibeonites said to him, There is not a problem  with us over silver nor gold with Saul, and with  his house;  and there is no  man for us to put to death from all Israel.
And he said, May it be -- the name  of God being blessed from the eon and unto the eon, for the wisdom and the might are his.
And responding the king said  to Daniel, In truth  your God,  is he God of gods, and lord of the kings, and uncovering mysteries, for you were able to uncover  this mystery.
And whereas they said, Allow the development of the roots of the tree in the ground;  your kingdom  abides to you from of which ever time you should know the heavenly authority.

And from  men shall banish they you, and with wild beasts  your dwelling shall be,  and grass as an ox they shall feed you, and seven seasons shall change over you, until of which time you shall know that the highest dominates the kingdom  of men, and to whom ever it seems good to give it.

And the ruler of the kingdom of the Persians stood right opposite me twenty days and one day. And behold, Michael, one of the rulers of the ones foremost, came to help me; and left I him there with the ruler of the kingdom of the Persians.
Joshua son of Nun, the one standing beside you, this one shall enter there; strengthen you him! for he shall allot it  to Israel and  your children, which you said to be for ravaging.
But you, the ones joining with the LORD your God, all live   today.
For what great nation in which there is to it a God near to them as the LORD our God is in all things in which should call upon we him?
For I die in  this land, and I shall not pass over  this Jordan.  But you shall pass over and shall inherit  this good land.
Not with your fathers the LORD ordained this covenant, but only with you; even you here all living today.

Esteem  your father  and  your mother!  in which manner the LORD your God gave charge to you,   that good should be to you, and that you may be a long time upon the earth, of which the LORD your God  gives to you.

But you here stand with me! and I shall speak to you all the commandments, and the ordinances, and the judgments, as many as you shall teach them. And let them do thus in the land which I give to them by lot!

but according to every  way which the LORD your God gave charge to you   to go by it, so that he should rest you, and good might be to you, and you should prolong your days upon the land which you shall inherit.
Not because you were more numerous than all the nations did the LORD prefer you, and chose you; for you are ones very few of all the nations.
And you shall remember the LORD your God,  that he gives to you strength  to produce the ability, that he should establish his covenant  which he swore by an oath to your fathers  as today.

Every  place which ever the track of your foot may tread   will be yours. From the wilderness and Antilebanon, and from the river of the great river Euphrates, and unto the sea  at the descent of the sun it will be  your boundaries.
For you are passing over the Jordan, entering to inherit the land which the LORD our God  gives to us by lot all the days. And you shall inherit and dwell in it.
You shall not eat it, that good should happen to you, and to your sons  after you, if you should do the good and the pleasing thing before the LORD your God.

Guard and hearken! and you shall do all the words which I give charge to you, that good should happen to you, and to your sons  through the eon, if you should do the pleasing and the good thing before the LORD your God.

And the Levite shall come, for there is no  portion for him nor lot with you, and the foreigner, and the orphan, and the widow  in  your cities,  and they shall eat and shall be filled; that the LORD your God should bless you   in all the works  of your hands,  in whichever ones  you should do.

for the LORD your God  blessed you in which manner he spoke to you; then you shall lend to many nations, but you shall not borrow; and you shall control  many nations, but of you they shall not control.

For in no way should the one lacking fail from  your land.  Because of this I give charge to you to do  this thing, saying, In opening, you shall open  your hands  to your needy brother,  and to the one wanting upon  your land.
And you shall remember that you were a servant  in the land of Egypt, and the LORD your God ransomed you   from there. On account of this I give charge to you to do  this thing.
For these nations which you inherit them, these shall hear prognostications and divinations; but to you the LORD your God did not so give.
Because of this I give charge to you  this thing, saying, Three cities you shall draw apart to yourself.
And you shall lift away the innocent blood  from you of them, if you should do the good and the pleasing thing before the LORD your God.

and shall penalize him a hundred shekels, and shall give them to the father of the young woman, because he brought a bad name upon an Israelite virgin,  and shall be she his wife; he shall not be able to send her away  at any time.

the man going to bed with her shall give  to the father of the young woman fifty double-drachmas of silver, and will be she his wife, because he humbled her; he will not be able  to send her away  at any time.
But if  you should not want to make a vow, it is not  a sin to you.
And you shall remember that you were a servant  in the land of Egypt, and the LORD your God ransomed you   from there. On account of this I give charge to you to do  this thing.
And you shall remember that you were a servant  in the land of Egypt. On account of this I give charge to you to do  this thing.
How he opposed you in the way, and beat your  rear guard, the ones tiring in your rear,  and you hungered and were tired. And he  feared not God.

May the LORD open to you his good treasury,   the heaven, to give the rain to your land  in its season;  to bless all the works  of your hands; and you shall lend to many nations, but you shall not borrow; and you shall yourself rule  many nations, but they shall not rule you.
The foreigner who is among you shall ascend over you upward and upward; but you shall go down lower and lower.
This one shall lend to you, but you shall not lend to this one;  this one will be for the head, and you will be for the tail.
And they will be for signs to you, and miracles, and among  your seed  until the eon.
Nor is it on the other side of the sea, saying, Who will pass through for us to the other side of the sea, and shall take for it us, and hearing it we will do it?

Be manly and be strong! Do not fear! nor should you be timid, nor should you be terrified from their face. For the LORD your God,  the one going before with you -- in no way should forsake he you, nor in any way should abandon he you.

And Moses called  Joshua, and said to him before all Israel, Be manly and strong! For you shall enter before the face  of this people,  into the land which the LORD swore by an oath to your fathers  to give to them, and you shall allot it to them.
And Moses gave charge  to Joshua son of Nun and said, Be manly and be strong! For you shall bring the sons of Israel into the land which the LORD swore by an oath to them, and he will be with you.
They sinned -- they are not children his; they are a scoffing -- a crooked generation, and one turning aside.
There is no  remembrance to the first things; and indeed to the last things being, there will not be  a remembrance of them, with the ones being born at the latter end.

And I turned and I beheld all the extortions, the ones happening under the sun. And behold, the tear of the ones being extorted, and there is not  one comforting them.   And by the hand of ones extorting them was by strength, and there is not  one comforting them.
Blessed is land your of which  your king  is a son of free nobles, and  your rulers  at a proper time shall eat in force, and shall not be ashamed.

that, All the nations of the kingdom know that every man or woman, who shall enter to the king, into the inner courtyard  uncalled, there is no  deliverance to him, except to whomever the king stretches out the golden rod, this one shall be delivered; and I have not been called to enter to the king -- they are these thirty days.

So that if you should disregard at this  time, from elsewhere help and protection will be to the Jews, but you and the house  of your father will be destroyed; and who knows if it be for  this occasion you reigned.
And the king said, What do you want, Esther, and what is request your? The worth unto half  of my kingdom will be to you.
And he showed to them his riches,  and the glory which the king invested in him, and how he made him to be preeminent, and to take the lead of the kingdom.

And Haman described  the things coming to pass to him to Zeresh  his wife,  and  to his friends. And the friends and wife said to him,  Since Mordecai is of the race of the Jews, and you began to be humbled before him, in falling you shall fall, in no way should you be able to defend against him, for the living God  is with him.

On account of this they call  these days Purim because of the lots, (for in their dialect they are called purim), because of the words  of this letter,  and as much as they suffered on account of it, and as much as happened to them and was stopped.
And in so far as  humbled they them by so much, they became many more. And they were strong exceedingly, exceedingly. And the Egyptians were abhorred of the sons of Israel.
And the male child maturing, she brought it to the daughter of Pharaoh. And he became to her for a son. And she named his name  Moses. Saying, For from the water took up I him.

And he said, Who placed you magistrate and judge over us? Is it that you want to do away with me in which manner you did away yesterday with the Egyptian? And Moses feared, and said, Surely thus this thing has become apparent.
And God said  to Moses, saying that, I will be with you. And this will be to you the sign that I send you in  your leading   my people from out of Egypt, and you shall serve  God in  this mountain.
And he shall speak  the things to the people, and he will be your mouth. But you will be to him the things for  God.
But you will say  to Pharaoh, Thus says the LORD -- son of my first-born, Israel.
And I will bring you into the land in which I stretched out  my hand to give it to Abraham, and Isaac, and Jacob. And I will give to it you by lot. I am the LORD.
But you shall speak to him all as much as I give charge to you. But Aaron  your brother  will speak to Pharaoh, so as to send the sons of Israel from out of his land.

Now then hasten to bring together  your cattle,  and as much as is to you in the plain! For all the men, and the cattle, as much as might be found in the plains, and what might not enter into a residence, and what hail should have fallen upon them,  it will come to an end.

And  our cattle shall go with us, and we shall not leave behind a hoof. For of them we shall take to serve the LORD our God. But we know not how we shall serve the LORD our God until the arriving of us there.
And Moses called the whole council of elders of Israel. And he said to them, Going forth, take to yourselves a sheep according to your kin, and sacrifice the passover!

But you shall take a bundle of hyssop, and having dipped it from the blood, of the blood by the door, you shall place it on the lintel and upon both of the doorposts, of the blood which is by the door; and you shall not go forth -- each from the door  of his house until morning.
And it shall be whenever  the LORD your God should bring you   into the land of the Canaanites, in which manner he swore by an oath to your fathers,  and shall give to it you,

And Moses yelled to the LORD. And the LORD showed to him a tree. And he put it into the water, and the water was sweetened. There he established with him ordinances and judgments, and there tested he him.
And in the morning you will see the glory of the LORD, in the hearing  of your grumbling against  God. And we, what are we that you complain against us?

And Moses said,  It is in the the LORD giving to you in the evening meats to eat, and bread loaves in the morning for fullness, on account of the LORD'S hearing   your grumbling which you complain against us. But we, what are we? For not is your grumbling against us,   but against  God.
And you shall be to me a royal priesthood, and a holy nation. These  words you shall say to the sons of Israel.

And the LORD said to Moses, Behold, I come to you in a column of cloud, that the people may hear me speaking to you, and may trust you into the eon. And Moses announced the words of the people to the LORD.
And Moses said  to  God, the people shall not be able to ascend to  mount  Sinai, for you testified to us, saying, Separate from the mountain and sanctify it!
Esteem  your father  and  your mother!  that good should happen to you, and that you may be a long time upon the good earth  which the LORD your God  gives to you.
You shall not make to yourselves gods of silver, and gods of gold; you shall not make to them yourselves.
And if  the master should give to him a wife, and she should bear to him, sons and daughters, the wife and the children shall be  his masters,  and he alone shall go forth by himself.
the owner of the pit shall pay; he shall give money to their owner, and the animal coming to an end will be his.

And if  it is made known that the bull is given to goring before  yesterday and before the third day, and they should have been testifying to its owner,  and he did not remove it, he shall pay bull for bull, but the bull coming to an end will be his.
And if  the thief should be found in the ditch, and being struck should die, it is not  murder to striking him.
And a foreigner you shall not maltreat, nor in any way  afflict. For you know the soul of the foreigner, for you yourselves were foreigners  in the land of Egypt.
And Moses shall approach  alone to  God, but they shall not approach, and the people shall not go up with them.
And Aaron said  to Moses, Be not provoked to anger, O master! for you know the impulse  of this people.

And Moses said  to the LORD, Behold, you say to me, Lead  this people! But you manifested not  to me whom you shall send along with me. But you said to me,  I know you above all, and you have favor with me.
And if you should give orders to the lawless one, and he should not turn from  his lawlessness,  nor  his unjust way;  that lawless one shall die in his iniquity,   and you  shall rescue your soul.
The ones saying, Have they not recently built the houses? This is the kettle, and are we the meats.

Be multiplied! I have made you as the rising of the field. And you were multiplied, and magnified, and entered into cities of cities.  Your breasts  erected, and  your hair  rose up; but you were naked and indecent.

And you, O son of man, say to the ruler of Tyre! Thus says Adonai the LORD; because your heart was raised up high,   and you said, I am the God, I inhabited the dwelling of God in the heart of the sea. But you are a man, and not God, and you made  your heart  as the heart of God.
But you, if you should forewarn the impious  of his way to turn from it, and he should not turn from  his way,  this one shall die in his impiety;  but you  rescued your own soul.
But your grape and your fruit my people shall eat O mountains of Israel;     for they are hoping  to come.

And he brought me there, and behold, a man. And  his appearance  was as the appearance of shining brass, and in  his hand  was a string measure of builders, and a reed measure. And set he them at the gate.
And the sacrifices, even the ones for a sin offering, and the ones for an ignorance offering, these they shall eat; and every separation offering in  Israel will be theirs.
their horses -- seven hundred thirty-six; mules -- their two hundred forty-five;
And I will put hatred between you and between the woman; and between  your seed  and between  her seed. He will give heed to your head, and you will give heed to his heel.

And to the woman he said, In multiplying I will multiply  your distresses,  and  your moanings.  In distresses you will bear children, and to  your husband   your submission,  and he will dominate you.
But you shall take to yourself from all of the foods which you shall eat, and you shall bring them together to yourself, and it shall be to you and to them to eat.
And the LORD God said  to Noah, Enter, you and all  your house  into the ark! for beheld I you as just before me among  this generation.

And the fear of you and  trembling will be upon all the wild beasts of the earth, upon all the winged creatures of the heaven, and upon all the things moving upon the earth, and upon all the fishes of the sea. Under your hands have given to I them you.
And every reptile which is living shall be to you for food; as I have given all vegetation of grass to you.
But you grow, and multiply, and fill the earth, and multiply upon the earth!
And the LORD said to Abram, Come forth from out of  your land,  and from  your kin,  and from the house  of your father, and come into the land which ever shall show to I you.

And a certain person of the ones being rescued coming, reported to Abram, to the traveler. And he dwelt at the oak  of Mamre the Amorite of the brother of Eshcol, and the brother of Aner, who were confederates  of Abram.
But you shall go forth to  your fathers  with peace, being entombed in a good old age.
And he took butter, and milk, and the young calf which he prepared, and placed it near to them, and they ate. And he stood beside them under the tree.
And Abimelech said   to Abraham, Behold,  my land is before you, which ever may please it you, dwell there!
But also the son  of this maidservant  I will make into a great nation, for he is your seed.
And he said, Take  your son,  the beloved, whom you loved --  Isaac! And go into the high land,  and offer him there as a whole offering upon one of the mountains! which ever may tell I you.
By me let it be, O lord, and hear me! The field and the cave  in it give to I you. Before all  of my fellow-countrymen I have given it to you. You entomb  your dead.
Not so O lord, for I heard four hundred double-drachmas of silver; but what ever may this be  between me and you? But you  entomb your dead!
And Isaac  was traveling over through the wilderness by the Well of the Vision. And he dwelt in the land  towards the south.
And the LORD appeared to him, and said, You should not go down into Egypt, but dwell in the land! which ever should tell I you.
And sojourn in  this land! And I will be with you, and I will bless you. For to you and  your seed  I will give all  this land. And I will establish  my oath, which I swore  to Abraham  your father.
And Isaac said to them, Why did you come to me; for you detested me, and you sent me from you?
And he made a banquet for them. And they ate and drank.
Now then, O my son,  hear me as I give charge to you!
And answering Isaac said  to Esau, If I made him master of you, and I made all his brothers   his servants, to support him -- with grain and wine then to you what may I do child?
And behold, I am with you, guarding you in  every way of which ever you may go. And I will return you to  this land, for no way would abandon I you until  my doing all as much as I spoke to you.
While he was speaking to them, and behold, Rachel the daughter of Laban comes with the sheep  of her father. For she grazed the sheep  of her father.
Give to me my wives, and  my children!  for whom I have served to you, that I might go forth. For you know the servitude which I have served you.
For there was little as much as was to you before me, and it was grown into a multitude; and the LORD blessed you by  my foot. Now then, when shall I also produce for myself a house?
And Laban said  to him, What shall give to I you? And Jacob said to him, You shall not give  to me anything. If you should do for me  this word, again I will tend  your flocks,  and guard them.
All the riches and the glory which the God of our father removed   will be ours and  our children's. Now then as much as God has said to you,   you do!
And he ran away, himself and  all of his. And he passed over the river, and he advanced unto the mountain of Gilead.
And the gifts went before  his face.  And he went to sleep  that night in the camp.
But he went forth in front of them, and did obeisance on the ground seven times, while approaching to his brother.
And the young man did not pass time  to do  this word, for he clung to the daughter of Jacob. And he was most honorable of all of the ones in the house  of his father.

And removing the garments of the widowhood from herself, she put around a lightweight covering, and bedecked herself, and sat by the gates of Enaim, which is in the byway of Timnath. For she knew that Shelah was older, but he did not give her to him as wife.
And  he said, I will send to you a kid of the goats from out of  my flocks.  And she said, You should give  a deposit until  you send it.

And  he said, What  deposit shall give to I you?  And she said, Your ring,  and the pendant, and the rod  in  your hand.  And he gave them to her, and he entered to her. And she conceived in the womb from him.
And Judah said, Let her have them, but lest at any time we should be ridiculed, I indeed sent  this kid, but you have not found her.
And Joseph recognized  his brothers,  but they did not recognize him.

You send one from you, and take  your brother! But you shall be taken away until  your sayings become apparent,   if you be truthful, or not. But if not, by the health of Pharaoh, assuredly you are spies.
If you are peaceable,  let one of your brothers be held  in the prison! but you yourselves proceed, and take back the purchase  of your provision!
And they did not know that Joseph heard,  for the translator was between them.
If  then, you send  our brother with us, we will go down, and we will buy foods for you.
Is this not  in which  my master drinks?   And he foretells an omen by it? You completed the wicked things which you have done.
And he said, And now as you say, so it will be. By whom ever the drinking cup should be found, will be my servant, and all you shall be pure.
And Joseph said, Let it not be to me to do  this thing. The man by whom was found with the drinking cup, he will be my servant. But all you ascend with safety to  your father.
And we said to the master, There is to us a father, an older man, and a younger child in his old age, and  his brother  died, and he alone was left behind  of his mother,  and his father loved him.
And you said to your servants,  If Your younger brother does not go down   with you, you shall not proceed to see  my face.

And we said, We will not be able to go down; but if then  our younger brother  goes down with us, we will go down; for not will we be able to see the face of the man,  if our younger brother  is not being with us.
And you shall dwell in the land of Goshen, and you will be near me, you and  your sons,  and the sons  of your sons,   your sheep  and  your oxen,  and as much as is yours.
